description_long: This is a small, rustic wooden cabin with a simple rectangular structure and a steeply pitched gable roof made of weathered wooden planks. The front facade features a single, centered doorway with no visible door, providing direct access to the interior. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontal wooden boards, supported at the corners by darker, rough-hewn timber posts. On one side, there is a small, square window with wooden bars, adding a touch of ventilation and light to the otherwise enclosed space. The roof has a slight overhang, offering minimal protection from rain. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian, hand-built shelter typical of rural or park settings.
description: A small, rustic wooden cabin with a steep gable roof, a single front doorway, and one small barred window on the side. The structure is made of weathered wood planks and timber posts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the centered open doorway, gable roof, and horizontal wooden siding.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long roof slope, a small square barred window, and the uninterrupted wooden siding.
description_view_2: Rear view with no openings, just the gable roof and plain wooden wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with only the wooden siding and roof visible, no windows or doors.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate overhang, constructed from wooden planks
attribute_window_arrangement: one small, square, barred window located on one side wall, asymmetrically placed
attribute_door_placement: single exterior doorway centered on the front facade
